meaning: n.预备演员,替角,代替之人v.练习做临时演员
examples: He was an understudy to Charlie Chaplin on a tour of the U.S.
A momentary hush; the orchestra leader varies his rhythm obligingly for her, and there is a burst of chatter as the erroneous news goes around that she is Gilda Gray's understudy from the Follies.
While the star is recuperating from her illness, her role will be played by her understudy.
Nancy Seabrooke was understudy for the role of Mrs Boyle for 6, 240 performances, appearing just 72 times.
Connacht fly-half Andy Dunne start at fly-half with Ulster's Niall O'Connor as his understudy on the bench.
